#!/usr/bin/env bash

Write a Bash script that compresses old .log files under a directory, dry-run by default.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

dir="${1:?usage: $0 directory days [--run]}"
days="${2:-7}"
mode="${3:---dry-run}"

find "$dir" -xdev -type f -name '*.log' -mtime +"$days" -print0 |
while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
 if [[ "$mode" == "--run" ]]; then
 gzip -n "$file"
 else
 echo "would gzip: $file"
 fi
done
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash

Write a Bash script that audits `snapd` status, result fields, and recent logs.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

unit="snapd"

echo "== $unit status =="
systemctl status "$unit" --no-pager || true

echo "== $unit result fields =="
systemctl show "$unit" -p ActiveState -p SubState -p Result -p ExecMainStatus

echo "== $unit recent logs =="
sudo journalctl -u "$unit" -n 100 --no-pager || true
```
This script creates a compact `snapd` triage report without modifying the service.

Write a Bash script that exits non-zero if any real filesystem is at least 75% full.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

limit=75
bad=0

while read -r fs blocks used avail pct mount; do
 pct="${pct%%%}"
 if [ "$pct" -ge "$limit" ]; then
 printf 'WARN %s is %s%% full on %s\n' "$fs" "$pct" "$mount"
 bad=1
 fi
done < <(df -P -x tmpfs -x devtmpfs | awk 'NR>1')

exit "$bad"
```
This script checks real filesystems against a 75% threshold and returns failure when any mount exceeds it.
